seo.title: PII Filtering for AI Agents in BeeAI Agents
seo.description: How to detect and fix pii filtering for ai agents in BeeAI agents. Framework-specific detection, fixes, and Vex automation.

content.intro: In BeeAI agents, PII Filtering failures manifest when sensitive data like emails, phone numbers, or SSNs leak into agent memory, tool calls, or persisted state due to inadequate preprocessing of user inputs and tool outputs. BeeAI's event-driven architecture and Granite model integration exacerbate this, as unfiltered LLM responses can propagate PII through memory buffers and tool parameters without validation. Long-running agent sessions compound the risk, with unbounded memory growth retaining PII across interactions.

howItManifests.scenario: User provides email in natural language query
howItManifests.symptom: Email appears unredacted in agent_memory.events and tool call parameters
howItManifests.impact: PII stored indefinitely in agent state, exposed via observability dashboards

howItManifests.scenario: Tool returns customer database with phone numbers
howItManifests.symptom: Phone numbers passed directly to Granite model without sanitization
howItManifests.impact: PII included in LLM context window, potential logging to external services

howItManifests.scenario: Agent persists conversation state containing addresses
howItManifests.symptom: Serialization includes raw PII in JSON state files
howItManifests.impact: Sensitive data stored in plaintext on disk, vulnerable to breaches

howItManifests.scenario: Granite model hallucinates synthetic PII in responses
howItManifests.symptom: Fake but realistic SSNs/credit card numbers in agent output
howItManifests.impact: Compliance violations and false positive alerts in monitoring systems

detection.name: Memory Event PII Scanner
detection.description: Registers BeeAI event listener to scan agent_memory events for PII patterns using regex
detection.code:
from beeai import Agent
from beeai.events import EventListener
import re

class PIIListener(EventListener):
    PII_PATTERNS = [
        r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b',
        r'\b\d{3}-\d{3}-\d{4}\b',
        r'\b\d{3}[\s-]?\d{2}[\s-]?\d{4}\b',
        r'\b\d{5}-\d{4}\b'
    ]

    def on_memory_event(self, event):
        content = event.data.get('content', '')
        for pattern in self.PII_PATTERNS:
            if re.search(pattern, content):
                print(f'🚨 PII detected in memory event: {event.event_id}')
                return False
        return True

agent = Agent()
agent.register_listener(PIIListener())

detection.name: Tool Call PII Validator
detection.description: Intercepts tool calls to validate parameters against PII regex before execution
detection.code:
from beeai.tools import ToolValidator
import re

class PIIToolValidator(ToolValidator):
    def validate_params(self, tool_name, params):
        param_str = str(params)
        email_pattern = r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b'
        if re.search(email_pattern, param_str):
            raise ValueError(f'PII detected in tool params for {tool_name}: {params}')
        return params

# Register globally
agent = Agent()
agent.tool_validator = PIIToolValidator()

detection.name: State Persistence PII Filter
detection.description: Scans agent state before JSON serialization to block PII persistence

fixes.name: Presidio PII Anonymizer Integration
fixes.description: Uses Microsoft's Presidio to automatically detect and anonymize PII before processing
fixes.code:
from presidio_analyzer import AnalyzerEngine
from presidio_anonymizer import AnonymizerEngine
from beeai.events import EventListener

analyzer = AnalyzerEngine()
anonymizer = AnonymizerEngine()

class PresidioPIIFilter(EventListener):
    def on_user_message(self, event):
        message = event.data['content']
        results = analyzer.analyze(text=message, language='en')
        anonymized = anonymizer.anonymize(text=message, analyzer_results=results)
        event.data['content'] = anonymized.text
        return event

agent = Agent()
agent.register_listener(PresidioPIIFilter())

# Install: pip install presidio-analyzer presidio-anonymizer

fixes.name: PII-Safe Tool Parameter Wrapper
fixes.description: Wraps all tool calls with automatic PII redaction using regex replacement

fixes.name: Memory TTL with PII Eviction
fixes.description: Configures agent memory with TTL and automatic PII eviction policy
fixes.code:
from beeai import AgentMemory
import re
from datetime import timedelta

class PIIAwareMemory(AgentMemory):
    def __init__(self, ttl_minutes=30):
        super().__init__()
        self.ttl = timedelta(minutes=ttl_minutes)
        self.pii_patterns = [
            r'\b[A-Za-z0-9._%+-]+@[A-Za-z0-9.-]+\.[A-Z|a-z]{2,}\b',
            r'\b\d{3}-\d{3}-\d{4}\b'
        ]

    def store_event(self, event):
        # Scrub PII before storage
        content = event['content']
        for pattern in self.pii_patterns:
            content = re.sub(pattern, '[PII_REDACTED]', content)
        event['content'] = content
        event['expires_at'] = self.now() + self.ttl
        super().store_event(event)

    def prune_expired(self):
        super().prune_expired()

memory = PIIAwareMemory(ttl_minutes=15)
agent = Agent(memory=memory)

vexAutomation.heading: Automating PII Filtering for AI Agents Detection with Vex
vexAutomation.description:
Vex integrates seamlessly with BeeAI's event system as a drop-in listener, automatically scanning all agent events for PII leakage across memory, tools, and persistence layers. It employs ML-powered PII detection that outperforms regex with 98% accuracy on 50+ entity types, providing real-time alerts and automatic remediation. Vex's agent reliability engine learns your specific PII patterns and BeeAI usage, creating custom detection rules that evolve with your agents.

The platform offers one-click remediation that injects PII filters into your BeeAI agents without code changes, plus compliance dashboards showing PII exposure trends. Vex's BeeAI integration captures full event traces including Granite model outputs, enabling root-cause analysis of PII leaks originating from LLM hallucinations or tool failures.
vexAutomation.code:
from vex_sdk import Vex
from beeai import Agent
from beeai.events import EventListener

# Install: pip install beeai-framework vex-sdk

class VexPIIListener(EventListener):
    def __init__(self):
        self.vex = Vex(api_key="your-vex-api-key")

    def on_agent_event(self, event):
        # Vex auto-scans for PII in all BeeAI events
        result = self.vex.scan_event(
            framework="beeai",
            event_type=event.event_type,
            payload=event.data,
            context={
                "agent_id": event.agent_id,
                "session_id": event.session_id
            }
        )
        
        if result.pii_detected:
            # Auto-remediate
            self.vex.remediate_pii(event.data, result.entities)
            print(f'✅ Vex blocked {len(result.entities)} PII entities')
        
        return result.is_safe

# Zero-config integration
agent = Agent()
agent.register_listener(VexPIIListener())

# Vex auto-generates dashboard at app.vex.ai/your-org
